name: Eddie Calhoun
abstract: Eddie Calhoun (November 13, 1921, in Clarksdale, Mississippi – January 27, 1994, in Paradise Lake, Michigan) was an American jazz double bassist. Calhoun was raised in Chicago, where he played with Dick Davis (1947–49) and Ahmad Jamal (1951–52).

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I collaborated with him on that -- called Shack in Hackensack.

about_quotes.speaker: Norman Simmons
about_quotes.evidence: But then when he formed this trio, Eddie Calhoun has got those maracas and he’s playing [scats]...
about_quotes.doc_title: Norman Simmons interviewed by Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 30, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: A guy named Eddie Calhoun was on bass... was in the band, too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
